questions.question: What is the key difference between localStorage and sessionStorage?
options: localStorage stores more data than sessionStorage
localStorage persists indefinitely while sessionStorage is cleared when the tab closes
sessionStorage works across all tabs while localStorage is tab-specific
questions.answer: localStorage persists indefinitely while sessionStorage is cleared when the tab closes
questions.explanation: localStorage data survives browser restarts and persists until explicitly cleared. sessionStorage is cleared when the browser tab is closed.

questions.question: Why are Web Workers useful?
options: They share the same memory as the main thread
They run heavy computation in a background thread without blocking the UI
questions.answer: They run heavy computation in a background thread without blocking the UI
questions.explanation: Web Workers run JavaScript in a separate thread, keeping the main UI thread responsive during CPU-intensive tasks like sorting large datasets.

questions.question: Which History API method navigates to a new URL without triggering a page reload?
questions.explanation: history.pushState() adds a new entry to the browser's history stack and updates the URL without reloading the page — the foundation of client-side routing.

questions.question: What does the Intersection Observer API allow you to do?
options: Detect when DOM elements enter or leave the viewport
questions.answer: Detect when DOM elements enter or leave the viewport
questions.explanation: Intersection Observer efficiently detects viewport visibility, enabling lazy loading, infinite scroll, and scroll-based animations without expensive scroll event listeners.

questions.question: What must you call in a dragover event handler to enable dropping?
questions.explanation: By default, browsers prevent dropping on most elements. Calling e.preventDefault() in the dragover event handler enables drop behavior.
